issues.title: validatePromoCode only relied on RLS to filter inactive codes
issues.description: The query for promo codes omitted .eq('is_active', true). RLS policy 'promo_codes_select_active' enforces this for the anon/authenticated roles, but if the client is ever initialized with service_role key (e.g., during testing or if a future code path changes the client) or the RLS policy is accidentally dropped, inactive/disabled coupon codes would be considered valid.
issues.fix: Added .eq('is_active', true) explicitly in the Supabase query.

issues.title: Discount amount computed entirely client-side; server never validates final total
issues.description: checkout() accepted a caller-supplied `total` and forwarded it directly to create-payment-intent. A malicious client could pass total=0.01 with any quantity, bypassing the promo discount calculation entirely. The promoCode was also not sent to the Edge Function, so even a correct server-side validation was impossible.
issues.fix: Added optional `promoCode` parameter to checkout() and _createPaymentIntent(), forwarded to Edge Function body as 'promoCode' key so the server can perform its own price verification.
issues.status: FIXED — NOTE: create-payment-intent Edge Function also needs to be updated to validate the promoCode server-side. This review covers the client contract change; the Edge Function update is a separate work item.

issues.title: userId falls back to empty string if auth state not loaded
issues.description: ref.read(currentUserProvider).valueOrNull?.id ?? '' produced an empty-string userId if the provider was still loading or the user was logged out. checkout_repository._createOrder then inserted an order row with user_id = '', which would fail the FK constraint against auth.users but only at the DB level, producing a confusing error. In the worst case (if the FK were ever relaxed), it would insert a phantom order.
issues.fix: Moved userId extraction before setState in _pay(), added explicit null/empty guard that shows _showPaymentFailedDialog and returns early.

issues.title: No CHECK constraint on discount_value — negative values possible
issues.description: A negative discount_value (e.g., -50 for percentage type) would result in calculatedDiscount being negative, which would *increase* the total instead of reducing it. Combined with the fact that the discount is computed client-side, this was an exploit path for price manipulation.
issues.fix: Added CHECK (discount_value > 0), CHECK (min_order_amount >= 0), CHECK (max_discount IS NULL OR max_discount > 0), CHECK (max_uses IS NULL OR max_uses > 0), CHECK (current_uses >= 0).

issues.title: current_uses increment is not atomic — race condition allows exceeding max_uses
issues.description: validatePromoCode reads current_uses and max_uses, then returns success. The increment of current_uses must happen after a successful checkout, but there was no atomic DB-level function to do this. Two concurrent users could both pass the max_uses check with the same code and both complete checkout, exceeding the limit.
issues.fix: Added DB function increment_promo_code_uses(code TEXT) that uses SELECT FOR UPDATE to lock the row and atomically check and increment. Only service_role is granted EXECUTE. Also added a trigger to enforce percentage discount_value <= 100.
issues.status: FIXED — NOTE: The Dart client (or Edge Function) must call rpc('increment_promo_code_uses', {'p_code': promoCode}) after a successful order insert.

issues.title: Nested async setState in quantity button callbacks
issues.description: _QtyButton onTap called setState() with a closure that also called the async method _applyCoupon(). _applyCoupon internally calls setState() asynchronously. Calling an async function inside a synchronous setState callback is a pattern that can cause 'setState() called during build' errors and is against Flutter guidelines.
issues.fix: Split into two operations: setState(() => _quantity--/++) followed by a separate call to _applyCoupon() outside the setState callback.

issues.title: handleChargeRefunded upsert on payments table may fail with NOT NULL violation
issues.description: The upsert body for the charge.refunded handler omitted user_id and amount, which are NOT NULL columns. If charge.refunded arrives before payment_intent.succeeded (edge case but possible in Stripe's delivery), the INSERT path of upsert would fail with a PostgreSQL NOT NULL constraint violation, causing the webhook handler to return 500 and trigger Stripe retries.
issues.fix: Replaced upsert with a SELECT to check for row existence, then a targeted UPDATE if the row exists. If the row does not exist, a warning is logged and execution continues. This eliminates the NOT NULL violation risk while preserving idempotency.

issues.title: error state Scaffold missing AppBar
issues.description: The dealAsync.error branch returned a Scaffold with no AppBar, inconsistent with the loading branch and the rest of the app. On error, the user had no back navigation.
issues.fix: Added AppBar with title 'Checkout' and replaced raw Text('Error: $e') with a user-friendly message.

issues.title: idx_promo_codes_code index is redundant with UNIQUE constraint
issues.description: PostgreSQL automatically creates a unique index for UNIQUE constraints. The explicit CREATE INDEX on (code) duplicates this index, wasting storage and slowing writes.
issues.fix: Not applied — the index comment serves as documentation. Removing it is a minor optimization; a future cleanup migration can DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_promo_codes_code.

issues.title: Silent fallback to /orders when couponId is null
issues.description: When couponId is null (e.g., the DB trigger auto_create_coupon hasn't run yet when the order detail is first fetched), the 'View My Coupon' button navigates to /orders instead of the specific coupon. The user may be confused why they were redirected. The root cause is a timing race between order insert and trigger execution — typically <100ms, but could be observable on slow connections.
issues.fix: Not applied — acceptable UX fallback. A follow-up improvement could poll/retry the orderDetailProvider until couponId is populated.

security_notes: Stripe webhook signature verification is correct — uses constructEventAsync with raw bytes. Good.
Webhook uses SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY only on the server side (Deno env). Not exposed to client. Good.
Client never sees service_role key — CheckoutRepository uses the user-authenticated Supabase client. Good.
RLS is enabled on promo_codes with correct SELECT-only policy for authenticated users. No INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policy means write operations are blocked for all non-service_role callers. Good.
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY and STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET are read from Deno.env, never hardcoded. Good.

api_contract_alignment.checkout_repository_to_create_payment_in: ALIGNED — amount, currency, dealId, userId fields match. promoCode is new addition (additive, backward-compatible).
api_contract_alignment.order_model_to_orders_table: ALIGNED — all fields map correctly. couponId nullable matches schema.
api_contract_alignment.promo_code_result_to_promo_codes_table: ALIGNED — all field names match column names.
